title: Chapter 1: Arriving in Alola
summary: You and your mother have just moved to the Alola region from Kanto. Professor Kukui greets you and introduces you to the island life. You meet Lillie and the mysterious Pokemon Nebby (Cosmog) at the Plank Bridge on Mahalo Trail.
objectives: Unpack in your new home on Route 1
Follow Professor Kukui to Iki Town
Rescue Nebby on the Mahalo Trail bridge
Receive the Sparkling Stone from Tapu Koko

keyItems.name: Sparkling Stone
keyItems.description: Given by the guardian deity Tapu Koko after you protect Nebby on the bridge. Later becomes your Z-Ring.

rewards: Introduction to the Alola region
Meet Professor Kukui, Lillie, and Hau
Sparkling Stone (future Z-Ring)
tips: Take your time exploring your house. You can interact with your Meowth for a fun animation. There are no Pokemon encounters yet, so just enjoy the story.

title: Chapter 2: Iki Town Festival
summary: At the festival in Iki Town, you choose your starter Pokemon from Professor Kukui: Rowlet, Litten, or Popplio. Island Kahuna Hala gives you your Pokedex and Rotom Dex. You battle your rival Hau for the first time and begin your journey.
objectives: Attend the festival at Iki Town
Choose your starter Pokemon (Rowlet, Litten, or Popplio)
Battle Hau in your first rival battle
Receive the Pokedex and Rotom Dex from Professor Kukui
Begin exploring Route 1

keyItems.name: Starter Pokemon
keyItems.description: Choose from Rowlet (Grass/Flying), Litten (Fire), or Popplio (Water)

keyItems.name: Pokedex / Rotom Dex
keyItems.description: Your talking Pokedex with a built-in map, powered by a Rotom

keyItems.name: Trainer Passport
keyItems.description: Your official ID as a Pokemon Trainer

rewards: Your first Pokemon (Level 5 starter)
Rotom Dex with map functionality
Access to Route 1 encounters

keyBattles.strategy: Hau picks the starter weak to yours. Just use your STAB move (Leafage, Ember, or Water Gun) to win easily.

tips: You can soft reset (L+R+START/SELECT) before choosing your starter to get a good nature. Adamant is ideal for Litten (physical attacker), Modest for Popplio (special attacker), and Adamant or Jolly for Rowlet.

title: Chapter 3: Verdant Cavern Trial
summary: Captain Ilima's trial in Verdant Cavern is your first Island Trial. You must defeat three Pokemon lurking in dens within the cave, then face the Totem Pokemon. In Ultra Sun, the Totem is Gumshoos; in Ultra Moon, it is Alolan Raticate.
objectives: Travel through Hau'oli City and Route 2 to reach Verdant Cavern
Defeat the three Pokemon hiding in the cave dens
Battle and defeat Totem Gumshoos (Ultra Sun) or Totem Raticate (Ultra Moon)
Receive the Normalium Z crystal
blockers: Must have spoken with Captain Ilima and received the trial challenge

keyItems.name: Normalium Z
keyItems.description: Z-Crystal that powers up Normal-type moves into Z-Moves

rewards: Normalium Z crystal
10 Great Balls from Ilima
Access to the back of Verdant Cavern (TM31 Brick Break, TM46 Thief)

keyBattles.strategy: Totem Gumshoos gets +1 Defense and can call Yungoos allies. Fighting-type moves are super effective. Use Makuhita (trade in Route 2 Pokemon Center) or a Pokemon with a Fighting move. Keep your HP above half since its boosted attacks hit hard.
Totem Alolan Raticate is Dark/Normal. Fighting-type moves deal 4x damage. Same strategy as Gumshoos but even more vulnerable to Fighting.

tips: Before entering the trial, make sure your team is at least level 12-13. Stock up on Potions at the Route 2 Pokemon Center. You cannot catch Pokemon during trials. After clearing the trial, go back and explore the blocked-off areas for great TMs.

title: Chapter 4: Melemele Grand Trial
summary: After completing the Verdant Cavern trial, Kahuna Hala challenges you to the Melemele Grand Trial in Iki Town. Hala specializes in Fighting-type Pokemon. Winning earns you the Fightinium Z and the ability to use Tauros as a Ride Pokemon.
objectives: Return to Iki Town after completing the Verdant Cavern trial
Receive your Z-Ring from Hala (crafted from the Sparkling Stone)
Battle Kahuna Hala in the Grand Trial
Receive the Fightinium Z and Ride Pager
blockers: Must have completed Captain Ilima's trial at Verdant Cavern

keyItems.name: Z-Ring
keyItems.description: Crafted from your Sparkling Stone by Hala. Allows you to use Z-Moves in battle.

keyItems.name: Fightinium Z
keyItems.description: Z-Crystal for Fighting-type Z-Moves

keyItems.name: Ride Pager
keyItems.description: Allows you to call Ride Pokemon like Tauros to smash rocks and traverse terrain

rewards: Z-Ring (enables Z-Moves)
Fightinium Z crystal
Ride Pager with Tauros Charge
Access to Akala Island via ferry

keyBattles.strategy: Hala's team is all Fighting-type. Flying types like Pikipek/Trumbeak, Rowlet/Dartrix are excellent. Psychic types like Drowzee also work. Crabrawler is his ace at level 16, holds Fightinium Z, and knows Power-Up Punch. Watch out for Makuhita's Fake Out on the switch-in turn.

tips: Make sure you have a Flying or Psychic type for this fight. If you chose Rowlet, this trial is a breeze. After winning, Tauros Charge lets you break rocks blocking items on previous routes. Go back and explore. The ferry to Akala is at Hau'oli Marina.

title: Chapter 5: Arriving on Akala
summary: You arrive on Akala Island and meet the Island Kahuna Olivia and the three trial captains: Lana, Kiawe, and Mallow. You explore Heahea City, traverse Route 4, visit Paniola Ranch, and prepare for the three Akala trials.
objectives: Take the ferry from Melemele to Akala Island
Meet Olivia and the trial captains in Heahea City
Explore Route 4 and Paniola Town
Visit Paniola Ranch for breeding and encounters
Battle Hau and encounter Gladion on Route 5
blockers: Must have completed the Melemele Grand Trial against Hala

keyItems.name: Stoutland Search
keyItems.description: Ride Pokemon that can sniff out hidden items on the ground

rewards: Access to Akala Island and its routes
Stoutland Search Ride Pokemon
Access to Paniola Ranch (Pokemon Nursery for breeding)
Access to the three Akala trials

keyBattles.strategy: Hau's team has grown but is still straightforward. Your starter should handle his easily. Ground types deal with Pikachu.
Gladion's Type: Null is a Normal-type with solid stats. Fighting moves are super effective. It's a tough fight at this point, so make sure your team is level 17-18.

tips: Paniola Ranch has the Pokemon Nursery (breeding). Leave two compatible Pokemon and walk around to hatch eggs. Route 4 has rare Eevee encounters (5%). The Amulet Coin can be found at Paniola Ranch from a man in the ranch house - grab it now for double money from trainer battles.

title: Chapter 6: Brooklet Hill Trial
summary: Captain Lana's Water-type trial at Brooklet Hill involves investigating mysterious splashing in several pools of water. Each pool reveals increasingly alarming Pokemon until you face the Totem Araquanid, a Water/Bug type with enhanced defenses.
objectives: Reach Brooklet Hill via Route 5
Investigate the three splashing pools during the trial
Defeat the Wishiwashi encounters at the first two pools
Battle and defeat Totem Araquanid at the final pool
Receive the Waterium Z crystal
blockers: Must have reached Brooklet Hill after events on Route 5

keyItems.name: Waterium Z
keyItems.description: Z-Crystal for Water-type Z-Moves

keyItems.name: Lapras Paddle
keyItems.description: Ride Pokemon that lets you surf across water

rewards: Waterium Z crystal
Lapras Paddle Ride Pokemon
Fishing Rod (can now fish in water spots)

keyBattles.strategy: Totem Araquanid gets +1 Speed and its Water Bubble ability doubles Water move damage while halving Fire damage taken. Electric and Rock moves are your best bet. It calls Masquerain or Dewpider as allies which can use Infestation to trap you. KO the ally quickly, then focus on the Totem. Grass types also work but beware of Bug Bite.

tips: Pikachu or Charjabug are excellent for this trial. Magneton if you've leveled Magnemite enough. After the trial, you unlock the Fishing Rod and Lapras Paddle for surfing. Revisit earlier water areas for new encounters like Magikarp (evolves into Gyarados at level 20).

title: Chapter 7: Wela Volcano Trial
summary: Captain Kiawe's Fire-type trial at Wela Volcano Park is a dance quiz. You watch Alolan Marowak perform dances and must spot the difference. Wrong answers trigger battles. The trial culminates in a fight against the Totem Alolan Marowak.
objectives: Reach Wela Volcano Park from Route 7
Watch the three Marowak dance performances
Correctly identify the differences in each round
Battle and defeat Totem Alolan Marowak
Receive the Firium Z crystal
blockers: Must have completed Captain Lana's trial at Brooklet Hill

keyItems.name: Firium Z
keyItems.description: Z-Crystal for Fire-type Z-Moves

rewards: Firium Z crystal
Charizard Glide Ride Pokemon (unlocked after this trial)

keyBattles.strategy: Totem Alolan Marowak is Fire/Ghost with a +1 Speed boost. It uses Shadow Bone and Flame Wheel. Water, Rock, Ground, Ghost, and Dark types are super effective. It calls Salazzle as an ally, which can use Toxic and Venoshock. Take out Salazzle first if it appears, then focus on Marowak. Avoid using Normal or Fighting moves (Ghost immunity/resistance).

tips: The dance quiz answers: first round the Hiker is the odd one, second round a Marowak faces a different direction, third round a completely different Pokemon (like a Totem Salazzle) appears. If you get a question wrong, you fight an extra trainer but still proceed. Water types like Gyarados or Wishiwashi (School Form at level 20+) dominate this trial.

title: Chapter 8: Lush Jungle Trial
summary: Captain Mallow's Grass-type trial in Lush Jungle has you collecting ingredients (Mago Berry, Honey, and Big Root) to cook a special meal. The aroma attracts the powerful Totem Lurantis, one of the toughest Totem Pokemon in the game.
objectives: Enter Lush Jungle from Route 5
Collect the Mago Berry from the berry tree area
Find the Honey near the Fomantis patch
Retrieve the Big Root from the cave section
Cook the ingredients at the trial site to lure out the Totem
Battle and defeat Totem Lurantis
Receive the Grassium Z crystal
blockers: Must have completed Captain Kiawe's trial at Wela Volcano Park

keyItems.name: Grassium Z
keyItems.description: Z-Crystal for Grass-type Z-Moves

rewards: Grassium Z crystal
Access to Route 8 and the rest of Akala
Mallow's recipe and trial completion stamp

keyBattles.strategy: Totem Lurantis is one of the hardest Totem battles. It gets +1 Speed and holds a Power Herb, allowing instant Solar Blade on turn 1 (a 125 BP Grass move). Its ally Kecleon can use Camouflage, and Comfey can heal with Floral Healing. Fire types are ideal - Fletchinder and Torracat resist Grass and hit super effectively. Flying types also work. Priority: KO allies immediately to prevent support. Use a Fire or Flying Z-Move if available.

tips: This is widely considered the hardest trial in Akala. Your team should be level 23-25 minimum. Fire/Flying type like Fletchinder is the MVP here. If you have Firium Z, use it on your strongest Fire move to one-shot or severely damage Lurantis. After the trial, the Moss Rock in Lush Jungle lets you evolve Eevee into Leafeon by leveling up near it.

title: Chapter 9: Akala Grand Trial
summary: After completing all three Akala trials, Kahuna Olivia challenges you to the Grand Trial at the Ruins of Life. Olivia specializes in Rock-type Pokemon, with her ace Lycanroc holding the Rockium Z. Winning grants you the Rockium Z and passage to Ula'ula Island.
objectives: Complete all three Akala trials (Lana, Kiawe, Mallow)
Meet Olivia at the Ruins of Life on the Akala coast
Battle Kahuna Olivia in the Grand Trial
Receive the Rockium Z crystal
Gain access to Ula'ula Island via ferry from Heahea City
blockers: Must have completed all three Akala Island trials (Brooklet Hill, Wela Volcano Park, Lush Jungle)

keyItems.name: Rockium Z
keyItems.description: Z-Crystal for Rock-type Z-Moves

rewards: Rockium Z crystal
Trainer Passport stamp (traded Pokemon obey up to Level 50)
Access to Ula'ula Island via ferry

keyBattles.strategy: Olivia's team is Rock-type but each has a secondary type to watch for. Anorith is Rock/Bug - use Water, Rock, or Steel moves. Lileep is Rock/Grass - Ice and Fighting work, but its Giga Drain can heal it. Lycanroc is her ace holding Rockium Z. Water, Grass, Ground, Fighting, and Steel are all super effective against Rock. A strong Water-type like Gyarados or Wishiwashi School Form can sweep. Watch for Lycanroc's Continental Crush Z-Move.

tips: Water and Grass types are your best friends for this battle. If you have a Gyarados or Wishiwashi (School Form), they can handle Olivia's entire team. Fighting types like Primeape from Route 3's Mankey evolution also work well. Bring a few Super Potions as Lycanroc hits hard with its Z-Move.

title: Chapter 10: Arriving on Ula'ula
summary: You arrive on Ula'ula Island, the largest island in Alola. Malie City is a Johto-inspired city with a beautiful garden and library. You explore the Malie Garden, encounter Professor Kukui as the Masked Royal, and meet the Aether Foundation's president Lusamine for the first time. Hau challenges you to a battle.
objectives: Take the ferry to Ula'ula Island and arrive in Malie City
Explore Malie Garden and battle your rival Hau
Visit the Malie Library and learn about the Legendary Pokemon
Meet the Aether Foundation members
Head to Route 10 toward Hokulani Observatory
blockers: Must have completed Kahuna Olivia's Grand Trial on Akala Island

keyItems.name: Professor's Mask
keyItems.description: You spot the Masked Royal at the Malie Garden stage, clearly Professor Kukui in disguise

rewards: Access to Ula'ula Island and Malie City shops
Malie Garden encounters (Poliwag, Psyduck, Goldeen via surfing)
Story progression with Aether Foundation lore

keyBattles.strategy: Hau's team has evolved. His Alolan Raichu is Electric/Psychic, so Ground types are ideal. His starter is fully evolved or close to it. Your team should be around level 27-29.

tips: Malie City has excellent shops including the Incense shop and the Salon for move tutoring. The Malie Library contains important lore about the Legendary Pokemon and Ultra Beasts. Stock up on Ultra Balls here as they become available at the Poke Mart. Route 10 has a bus that takes you up to Hokulani Observatory.

title: Chapter 11: Hokulani Observatory Trial
summary: Captain Sophocles runs the Electric-type trial at the Hokulani Observatory atop Mount Hokulani. The trial involves answering audio quiz questions about Pokemon cries while fending off charger Pokemon that attack you. The Totem Togedemaru awaits as the final challenge.
objectives: Take the bus from Route 10 to Mount Hokulani
Enter the Hokulani Observatory and begin Sophocles' trial
Answer the audio quiz questions correctly
Defeat the charger Pokemon that attack during the quiz
Battle and defeat Totem Togedemaru
Receive the Electrium Z crystal
blockers: Must have reached Route 10 and taken the bus to Hokulani Observatory

keyItems.name: Electrium Z
keyItems.description: Z-Crystal for Electric-type Z-Moves

keyItems.name: Steelium Z
keyItems.description: Found on the observatory grounds after the trial

rewards: Electrium Z crystal
Steelium Z (from Molayne on the observatory grounds)
Access to Route 11 and beyond

keyBattles.strategy: Totem Togedemaru is Electric/Steel with +1 Defense boost. It calls Skarmory (Lv 32) and Dedenne (Lv 31) as allies. Ground types are essential here since Ground is 4x effective against Electric/Steel. Mudsdale or Mudbray with Earthquake can one-shot it. Beware of its Zing Zap which can flinch and its Lightning Rod ability which absorbs Electric moves. Take out Skarmory first as it resists Ground with its Flying type.

tips: Ground types absolutely dominate this trial. If you have a Mudsdale, Krookodile, or even a Dugtrio, this fight is straightforward. Togedemaru's Steel typing removes its Electric weakness, making Ground the only super-effective option. Be careful of the Skarmory ally which is immune to Ground. Use Fire or Electric moves on Skarmory. Molayne gives you Steelium Z after the trial.

title: Chapter 12: Thrifty Megamart Trial
summary: Captain Acerola's Ghost-type trial takes place in the Abandoned Thrifty Megamart on Route 14. You must use the Poke Finder camera to photograph ghost Pokemon lurking in the abandoned store. The terrifying Totem Mimikyu awaits in the back room.
objectives: Travel to Route 14 and the Abandoned Thrifty Megamart
Enter the trial and use the Poke Finder to photograph ghosts
Investigate all the disturbances in the store
Photograph the final ghost in the back room
Battle and defeat Totem Mimikyu
Receive the Ghostium Z crystal
blockers: Must have completed Captain Sophocles' trial at Hokulani Observatory

keyItems.name: Ghostium Z
keyItems.description: Z-Crystal for Ghost-type Z-Moves

rewards: Ghostium Z crystal
Access to continue toward Tapu Village and Route 15

keyBattles.strategy: Totem Mimikyu is Ghost/Fairy with ALL stats boosted by +1. It calls Banette (Lv 32) and Jellicent (Lv 33) as allies. Mimikyu's Disguise ability blocks the first hit it takes, so you need at least two attacks to start dealing damage. Steel types are ideal since they resist both Ghost and Fairy. Use a multi-hit move like Rock Blast to break Disguise and deal damage in one turn. Avoid Normal and Fighting moves (Ghost immunity). Take out Jellicent first as it can use Water Spout.

tips: Totem Mimikyu is one of the most feared Totem Pokemon. Its Disguise means you waste a turn breaking it. Multi-hit moves like Bullet Seed, Rock Blast, or Icicle Spear bypass Disguise. Steel types like Magnezone resist both STAB types. Ghost or Dark types hit super effectively but take super effective damage back. Bring Full Restores. The abandoned store atmosphere is genuinely creepy.

title: Chapter 13: Ula'ula Grand Trial
summary: Kahuna Nanu specializes in Dark-type Pokemon and battles you on Route 15 as you head toward the Aether Foundation. His team includes Sableye, Krokorok, and his ace Alolan Persian with Darkinium Z. Team Skull's activity escalates and Lillie is kidnapped by the Aether Foundation.
objectives: Resolve the Team Skull situation at Po Town
Battle Guzma, the Team Skull boss, at the Shady House
Return to Route 15 and face Kahuna Nanu
Defeat Nanu in the Grand Trial to earn the Darkinium Z
Learn that Lillie has been taken to Aether Paradise
blockers: Must have completed both Ula'ula trials (Sophocles and Acerola)
Must have completed events at Po Town

keyItems.name: Darkinium Z
keyItems.description: Z-Crystal for Dark-type Z-Moves

keyItems.name: Buginium Z
keyItems.description: Found in the Shady House after defeating Guzma

rewards: Darkinium Z crystal
Trainer Passport stamp (traded Pokemon obey up to Level 65)
Buginium Z from Guzma's room
Story progression toward Aether Paradise

keyBattles.strategy: Guzma leads with Golisopod (Bug/Water) which has Emergency Exit, automatically switching out when its HP drops below 50%. Use Electric or Flying moves. Masquerain is Bug/Flying, weak to Rock, Electric, Fire, and Ice. Pinsir is pure Bug. Flying and Fire types handle his whole team well.

Nanu's Sableye is Dark/Ghost with no 4x weaknesses (only Fairy is super effective). Krokorok is Ground/Dark, weak to Water, Grass, Ice, Bug, Fighting, and Fairy. His ace Alolan Persian holds Darkinium Z and knows Dark Pulse, Power Gem, and Fake Out. Fairy types are the best counter to his team overall. Fighting types work against Persian and Krokorok but not Sableye. A strong Fairy like Ribombee can sweep.

tips: The Po Town raid has many Team Skull grunts to battle, so make sure you have healing items. Guzma's Golisopod is tricky with Emergency Exit. Nanu's battle level is higher than expected at 43-44, so grind if needed. Fairy types are the MVP for Nanu. After this chapter, the story accelerates dramatically.

title: Chapter 14: Aether Foundation & Ultra Space
summary: You infiltrate Aether Paradise to rescue Lillie and Nebby. President Lusamine has been using Nebby's power to open Ultra Wormholes and summon Ultra Beasts. You battle through Aether Foundation employees, face Guzma again, and ultimately confront Lusamine. In Ultra Sun/Moon, Necrozma absorbs Nebby (now Solgaleo/Lunala) and you must pursue them to Ultra Megalopolis.
objectives: Travel to Aether Paradise via ferry
Battle through Aether Foundation employees on multiple floors
Defeat Faba and the other Aether Branch Chiefs
Battle Guzma again in the lower levels
Confront Lusamine and witness the Ultra Wormhole opening
Travel to Ultra Megalopolis to face Necrozma
Defeat Ultra Necrozma and rescue Nebby
blockers: Must have completed all Ula'ula Grand Trial and story events

keyItems.name: Master Ball
keyItems.description: Given by Gladion at Aether Paradise, guaranteed catch on any Pokemon

keyItems.name: Cosmog
keyItems.description: Nebby evolves from Cosmog into Cosmoem, then into Solgaleo (Ultra Sun) or Lunala (Ultra Moon)

rewards: Master Ball from Gladion
Solgaleo (Ultra Sun) or Lunala (Ultra Moon) joins your team
Access to Poni Island
Story progression to the final island

keyBattles.strategy: Faba uses Psychic-types. Dark, Bug, and Ghost types are super effective. A strong Dark-type like Zoroark or Honchkrow can sweep his team.
Same strategy as before but higher levels. Flying and Fire types are strong. Rock types handle his whole team well. Watch for Golisopod's Emergency Exit and First Impression.

Lusamine has a diverse team. Clefable is Fairy, Lilligant is Grass, Lopunny is Normal, Milotic is Water, Bewear is Normal/Fighting. No single type covers everything. Poison types handle Clefable and Lilligant. Milotic has Recover, so burst it down. Bewear hits incredibly hard with Fluffy ability.

Ultra Necrozma is Psychic/Dragon with absurdly high stats (all base stats above 130). Its Neuroforce ability boosts super-effective moves. This is possibly the hardest mandatory battle in any Pokemon game. Strategy: use Toxic stall with a Sturdy Pokemon, use a Focus Sash Pokemon with Toxic, or use Mimikyu's Disguise to survive a hit and set up. A Zoroark disguised as a Fairy type can bait Necrozma into using a non-super-effective move. Level your team to at least 55+.

tips: Ultra Necrozma at level 60 is the hardest fight in the game. Prepare extensively. Strategies that work: Mimikyu with Swords Dance, Focus Sash Zoroark, Toxic stalling with Mudsdale/Toxapex, or simply overleveling to 60+. Save your Master Ball for later roaming legendaries or Ultra Beasts. After this chapter, Nebby joins your team as Solgaleo/Lunala.

title: Chapter 15: Poni Island Trials
summary: You arrive on Poni Island, the smallest and most remote island. Captain Mina's Fairy-type trial is unique - you must battle multiple trial captains across Alola to collect colored petals and create a Rainbow Flower. The Dragon-type trial in Vast Poni Canyon has no captain and culminates in a battle against the fearsome Totem Kommo-o. Kahuna Hapu challenges you to the final Grand Trial.
objectives: Arrive on Poni Island at Seafolk Village
Begin Mina's trial and collect petals from trial captains across Alola
Defeat Totem Ribombee after assembling the Rainbow Flower
Receive the Fairium Z crystal
Battle Kahuna Hapu at Exeggutor Island for the Groundium Z
Enter Vast Poni Canyon and complete the Dragon-type trial
Defeat Totem Kommo-o and receive the Dragonium Z
blockers: Must have completed events at Aether Paradise and Ultra Megalopolis

keyItems.name: Fairium Z
keyItems.description: Z-Crystal for Fairy-type Z-Moves, from Mina's trial

keyItems.name: Groundium Z
keyItems.description: Z-Crystal for Ground-type Z-Moves, from Hapu's Grand Trial

keyItems.name: Dragonium Z
keyItems.description: Z-Crystal for Dragon-type Z-Moves, from Vast Poni Canyon trial

rewards: Fairium Z crystal
Groundium Z crystal
Dragonium Z crystal
Trainer Passport stamp (traded Pokemon obey up to Level 80)
Access to Mount Lanakila and the Pokemon League

keyBattles.strategy: Totem Ribombee is Bug/Fairy with ALL stats boosted by +1. It calls Blissey (Lv 53) and Pelipper (Lv 52) as allies. Ribombee is fast and hits hard with Dazzling Gleam and Pollen Puff. Steel and Poison types are ideal, resisting both STAB types. Fire types also work. Take out Blissey first as it provides healing support. Pelipper sets up Rain with Drizzle.

Hapu is all Ground types. Water, Grass, and Ice moves are generally super effective. Golurk is Ground/Ghost - use Water, Grass, Dark, Ghost, or Ice. Gastrodon is Ground/Water with only Grass as a weakness (4x). Flygon is Ground/Dragon, 4x weak to Ice. Mudsdale is her ace holding Groundium Z. A Pokemon with Grass or Ice coverage handles most of her team.

Totem Kommo-o is Dragon/Fighting with ALL stats boosted by +1. Its allies are Scizor (Lv 46) and Noivern (Lv 48). Fairy types deal 4x damage and are immune to Dragon, making them the absolute best counter. Bring Ribombee, Mimikyu, Gardevoir, or any strong Fairy. Ice and Flying also hit super effectively. Watch out for Scizor's Bullet Punch priority.

tips: Mina's trial is a scavenger hunt that takes you across all islands to battle captains. It is time-consuming but the battles give good EXP. Hapu's Gastrodon has only one weakness (Grass 4x), so bring a strong Grass move. For Totem Kommo-o, a single Fairy-type can solo the fight. Ribombee with Dazzling Gleam is the easiest answer.

title: Chapter 16: Mount Lanakila & Pokemon League
summary: You climb Mount Lanakila, the tallest peak in Alola, battling through ice caves and snowy paths. Gladion challenges you one final time near the summit. At the top, the newly constructed Pokemon League awaits. You must defeat the Elite Four (Molayne, Olivia, Acerola, Kahili) and then face your rival Hau as the Champion.
objectives: Enter Mount Lanakila from Tapu Village
Navigate the ice caves and mountain paths
Defeat Gladion in a rival battle near the summit
Reach the Pokemon League at the peak
Defeat Elite Four Molayne (Steel)
Defeat Elite Four Olivia (Rock)
Defeat Elite Four Acerola (Ghost)
Defeat Elite Four Kahili (Flying)
Defeat Champion Hau and become the Alola Champion
blockers: Must have completed all Grand Trials and Island Trials
Must have completed events at Vast Poni Canyon

keyItems.name: Champion Title
keyItems.description: You become the first Champion of the Alola Pokemon League

rewards: Title of Alola Champion
Hall of Fame entry for your team
Access to post-game content
Ability to defend your title against challengers

keyBattles.strategy: Gladion's Silvally holds a memory disc matching your starter's weakness type. Crobat is Poison/Flying - use Electric, Ice, Rock, Psychic. Lucario is Fighting/Steel - use Fire, Ground, or Fighting. Silvally varies but is strong. Have broad type coverage ready.

Fire and Ground types handle most Steel types. A strong Fire-type sweeper like Incineroar or Arcanine can blast through. Magnezone's Sturdy ability means it survives one hit. Metagross hits hard with Meteor Mash. Ground moves hit 4 of 5 Pokemon super effectively.

Water and Fighting types dominate Olivia. Cradily has no Rock weakness (Rock/Grass) but is weak to Ice, Bug, Fighting, and Steel. Probopass is Rock/Steel, 4x weak to Ground and Fighting. A strong Water-type with good coverage moves can sweep.

Dark types resist Ghost moves and hit super effectively. Dhelmise is Ghost/Grass, Froslass is Ice/Ghost, Palossand is Ghost/Ground. A strong Dark-type like Honchkrow, Zoroark, or Alolan Muk handles most of her team. Watch for Froslass's Blizzard and Palossand's Shore Up recovery.

Electric types are the best counter. Ice and Rock also work well. Hawlucha is Fighting/Flying, weak to Electric, Ice, Flying, Psychic, and Fairy. Oricorio Baile is Fire/Flying, weak to Rock, Water, Electric. Mandibuzz is Dark/Flying. An Alolan Raichu or Magnezone can sweep most of her team.

Hau's team depends on your starter choice. His starter is the one strong against yours (level 60). His Eeveelution also varies. Alolan Raichu is Electric/Psychic - Ground types shut it down. Crabominable is Fighting/Ice. Prepare for a long, varied battle with diverse type coverage on your team.

tips: Stock up on Full Restores, Max Revives, and Max Potions before entering the League. You cannot leave once you start. Your team should be level 55-60. The Elite Four can be fought in any order. Consider bringing: a Fire type for Molayne, a Water type for Olivia, a Dark type for Acerola, an Electric type for Kahili, and broad coverage for Hau. You can rebattle the Elite Four after becoming Champion to grind money and EXP.

title: Chapter 17: Post-Game
summary: After becoming Champion, a wealth of post-game content opens up. The Ultra Wormhole Ride lets you travel to alternate dimensions to catch Legendary Pokemon from past games and Ultra Beasts. Team Rainbow Rocket invades Aether Paradise in Episode RR, bringing together villain leaders Giovanni, Archie, Maxie, Cyrus, Ghetsis, and Lysandre. The Battle Tree on Poni Island offers high-level competitive battles.
objectives: Defend your Champion title against challengers at the Pokemon League
Unlock Ultra Warp Ride to travel through Ultra Wormholes
Catch Ultra Beasts across Alola (missions from Looker and Anabel)
Complete Episode RR: defeat Team Rainbow Rocket at Aether Paradise
Battle all six villain leaders (Giovanni, Archie, Maxie, Cyrus, Ghetsis, Lysandre)
Challenge the Battle Tree on Poni Island
Collect all Zygarde Cells to assemble Zygarde
Catch the Guardian Deities (Tapu Koko, Tapu Lele, Tapu Bulu, Tapu Fini)
blockers: Must have become the Alola Champion

keyItems.name: Ultra Warp Ride
keyItems.description: Ride Solgaleo/Lunala through Ultra Wormholes to find Legendary Pokemon and Ultra Beasts in alternate dimensions

keyItems.name: Zygarde Cube
keyItems.description: Collect all 100 Zygarde Cells and Cores scattered across Alola to assemble Zygarde (10%, 50%, or Complete Forme)

rewards: Legendary Pokemon from past games via Ultra Wormholes (Mewtwo, Ho-Oh, Lugia, Groudon, Kyogre, Dialga, Palkia, etc.)
Ultra Beasts (Nihilego, Buzzwole, Pheromosa, Xurkitree, Kartana, Celesteela, Guzzlord, Blacephalon, Stakataka, Poipole)
All four Tapu guardians catchable at their respective ruins
Episode RR completion rewards and story resolution
Battle Tree exclusive items (Mega Stones, rare items, BP)

keyBattles.strategy: Giovanni's Mewtwo is the biggest threat at level 70. Bug, Ghost, and Dark types hit Mewtwo super effectively. His Ground-type team members are weak to Water, Grass, and Ice. A strong Fairy or Dark type plus a Water type can handle his entire team.
Ghetsis has a diverse team. Hydreigon is Dragon/Dark, 4x weak to Fairy. Eelektross has Levitate so Ground moves do not work. Fighting types handle Bisharp and Bouffalant. Bring diverse type coverage.

tips: Ultra Wormholes are the main post-game attraction. Travel further through the wormhole mini-game to find rarer Legendary Pokemon. The color of the wormhole determines what type of world you enter: Red = Cliff World (Groudon, Ho-Oh, etc.), Blue = Water World (Kyogre, Lugia, etc.), Green = Cave World (Zekrom, Dialga, etc.), Yellow = Field World (Raikou, Cresselia, etc.). Episode RR villain leaders each have one of their version's mascot Legendary. The Battle Tree at Poni Island lets you team up with Red and Blue from Kanto.
